page: Microsoft Azure E4ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the EPSv5 server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for standard compute and memory tasks, with some limitations in specialized workloads. The instance achieves average results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, alongside average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes also perform in the average range at 50.14 GB/sec and 29.77 GB/sec, respectively. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core tests, and Passmark CPU metrics. Cryptography and LLM inference tasks show poor performance, with the 135M model prompt processing reaching 159.24 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4ps_v5 offers a high-density memory profile on an ARM architecture, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance vector extensions or local NVMe storage. The instance is well-suited for database operations, text processing, and general-purpose memory-optimized workloads. It is less suitable for intensive cryptographic operations, machine learning inference, or heavy multi-threaded compute tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the EPSv5 family, configured with 4 dedicated physical cores on a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data shows average performance in single-core stress-ng tests, large-block memory bandwidth, and text processing, though it exhibits weaker results in multi-threaded decompression, cryptography, and LLM inference. Qualitatively, the E4ps_v5 provides a cost-efficient memory-optimized profile on ARM architecture, making it suitable for database operations, text processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring a larger memory footprint without intensive compute demands.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 32 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, optimized for memory-intensive database and text processing workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 4 dedicated Ampere Altra cores and 32 GB of RAM, designed for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering a high memory-to-core ratio for database operations and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor with 4 dedicated cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Average performance in database operations, text processing, and single-core benchmarks
